items.title: D-Judge: Disrupting Multi-Turn Jailbreaks using Semantics-Preserving Output Rewriting
items.abstract: Multi-turn jailbreak attacks pose a growing threat to large language model (LLM) safety because they exploit feedback from auxiliary judge models to iteratively refine prompts toward harmful goals. Existing defenses largely detect or block unsafe content at individual turns or at the final response, leaving the judge-driven refinement loop intact and allowing attackers to extract informative feedback from intermediate interactions. We introduce D-Judge, a semantics-preserving output rewriting defense that intervenes directly in this loop by rewriting the victim LLM's responses before they are evaluated by the attacker's judge. By misaligning the judge's feedback signal without changing the meaning of the original response, D-Judge derails the attacker's prompt-refinement process, causing subsequent queries to be optimized against a distorted signal of attack progress. To improve D-Judge's ability to produce such rewrites, we construct a dataset of semantically equivalent response pairs that induce different judge-assigned harmfulness scores, and use it for supervised fine-tuning followed by direct preference optimization. Experiments on HarmBench show that D-Judge reduces the success rate of state-of-the-art multi-turn jailbreaks while preserving performance on benign benchmarks.
items.llm_evidence_en: defense against multi-turn jailbreaks via output rewriting
items.llm_evidence_cn: 通过输出重写防御多轮越狱
items.llm_evidence: 通过输出重写防御多轮越狱
items.canonical_evidence: 通过输出重写防御多轮越狱
items.llm_tldr_en: D-Judge disrupts multi-turn jailbreak attacks by rewriting the victim LLM's responses before the attacker's judge evaluates them, misaligning feedback without changing semantics.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 多轮越狱攻击利用辅助评判模型反馈迭代优化提示。现有防御仅拦截不安全内容，未破坏评判循环。D-Judge通过语义保持的输出重写，干扰评判反馈信号，从而瓦解攻击。实验显示该防御有效降低攻击成功率。
items.llm_tldr: 多轮越狱攻击利用辅助评判模型反馈迭代优化提示。现有防御仅拦截不安全内容，未破坏评判循环。D-Judge通过语义保持的输出重写，干扰评判反馈信号，从而瓦解攻击。实验显示该防御有效降低攻击成功率。

items.title: Defenses & Enablers For Skill Injection Attacks on Terminal Based Agents
items.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on reusable skills i.e. documents describing task-specific procedures. However, this introduces a new attack surface for agents to manage. We study two complementary directions for this threat. First, we evaluate guardian-based defenses: an intermediary LLM agent that acts as a mediator for skill file access (dynamic guardian) or pre-rewrites these files at build time (static guardian). Across three LLM agent families, our guardians cut attack success rate (ASR) by well over half while preserving task utility. Second, we stress test them through attack reframing using four attacks that preserve the malicious instruction but change the phrasing. For non-guardian setup, the reframing pushes the ASR up to 81.4\%, but the dynamic guardian brings it down to 18.6\%, showing that real-time mediation is a robust defense.
items.llm_evidence_en: proposes guardian-based defenses for skill injection attacks on LLM agents
items.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于守护者的LLM智能体技能注入攻击防御方法
items.llm_evidence: 提出基于守护者的LLM智能体技能注入攻击防御方法
items.canonical_evidence: 提出基于守护者的LLM智能体技能注入攻击防御方法
items.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces dynamic and static guardian defenses for LLM agents that cut skill injection attack success rate by over half while preserving utility.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作针对LLM智能体依赖的可重用技能文件引入的注入攻击面，提出了两种守护者防御：动态守护者作为访问中介，静态守护者预处理文件。在三个智能体家族上测试，防御方法将攻击成功率降低一半以上，同时保持任务效用。此外还通过攻击重构测试了防御鲁棒性。直接贡献于智能体安全机制。
items.llm_tldr: 该工作针对LLM智能体依赖的可重用技能文件引入的注入攻击面，提出了两种守护者防御：动态守护者作为访问中介，静态守护者预处理文件。在三个智能体家族上测试，防御方法将攻击成功率降低一半以上，同时保持任务效用。此外还通过攻击重构测试了防御鲁棒性。直接贡献于智能体安全机制。
